policies.label: Climate change (SW-CC-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that conserve, restore or enhance habitats that provide flood defence or carbon sequestration will be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on habitats that provide a flood defence or carbon sequestration ecosystem service must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant d) compensate for significant adverse impacts that cannot be mitigated.
consideration: This project will not affect any habitats that provide flood defence or carbon sequestration services. The pontoon is a small floating structure within an existing harbour basin and will have no bearing on coastal flood defences.

policies.label: Climate change (SW-CC-3)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals in the south west marine plan areas, and adjacent marine plan areas, that are likely to have significant adverse impacts on coastal change, or on climate change adaptation measures inside and outside of the proposed project areas, should only be supported if they can demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant.
consideration: The project will not have any significant impact on coastal change or climate adaptation. The floating nature of the pontoon means it automatically adjusts to any future changes in sea level.

policies.label: Co-existence (SW-CO-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that optimise the use of space and incorporate opportunities for co-existence and co­ operation with existing activities will be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on, or displace, existing activities must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant. If it is not possible to mitigate significant adverse impacts, proposals must state the case for proceeding.
consideration: We are committed to operating alongside other harbour users in a cooperative and considerate manner. The pontoon location has been agreed with the harbour authority to minimise any conflict with existing users. We will liaise with the harbour master on an ongoing basis regarding seasonal adjustments to our operations if required.

policies.label: Cross-border co-operation (SW-CBC-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals must consider cross-border impacts throughout the lifetime of the proposed activity. Proposals that impact upon one or more marine plan areas or terrestrial environments must show evidence of the relevant public authorities (including other countries) being consulted and responses considered.
consideration: This project has no cross-border impacts into other marine plan areas. Teignmouth Harbour is entirely within the South West Marine Plan area.

policies.label: Cumulative effects (SW-CE-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals which may have adverse cumulative effects with other existing, authorised, or reasonably foreseeable proposals must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse cumulative and/or in-combination effects so they are no longer significant.
consideration: We are not aware of any other proposed works in the immediate vicinity that could give rise to cumulative effects with this project. The harbour operates a busy leisure and commercial fishing function already and the addition of a small pontoon is not considered to materially add to any existing cumulative pressures.

policies.label: Aquaculture (SW-AQ-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals enabling the provision of infrastructure for sustainable aquaculture and related industries will be supported.
consideration: The project does not involve aquaculture and will have no impact on aquaculture activities.

policies.label: Cables (SW-CAB-1)
policies.policyInfo: Preference should be given to proposals for cable installation where the method of protection is burial. Where burial is not achievable, decisions should take account of protection measures for the cable that may be proposed by the applicant. Where burial or protection measures are not appropriate, proposals should state the case for proceeding without those measures.
consideration: The project does not involve any cable laying activities.

policies.label: Cables (SW-CAB-3)
policies.policyInfo: Where seeking to locate close to existing subsea cables, proposals should demonstrate compatibility with ongoing function, maintenance and decommissioning activities relating to the cable.
consideration: We have checked the available marine infrastructure mapping and are not aware of any subsea cables in the immediate vicinity of the proposed pontoon location. The harbour master has confirmed no known cables are present in the installation area.

policies.label: Dredging and disposal (SW-DD-1)
policies.policyInfo: In areas of authorised dredging activity, including those subject to navigational dredging, proposals for other activities will not be supported unless they are compatible with the dredging activity.
consideration: No dredging activity is involved in this project.

policies.label: Dredging and disposal (SW-DD-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that cause significant adverse impacts on licensed disposal sites should not be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on licensed disposal sites must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant. If it is not possible to mitigate the significant adverse impacts, proposals must state the case for proceeding.
consideration: No impact on licensed disposal sites is anticipated.

policies.label: Dredging and disposal (SW-DD-3)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals for the disposal of dredged material must demonstrate that they have been assessed against the waste hierarchy. Where there is the need to identify new dredge disposal sites, including alternative use sites, proposals should be supported if they conform to best practice and guidance.
consideration: No dredged material will be produced as part of this project.

policies.label: Infrastructure (SW-INF-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals for appropriate marine infrastructure which facilitates land-based activities, or land-based infrastructure which facilitates marine activities (including the diversification or regeneration of sustainable marine industries), should be supported.
consideration: The installation of the pontoon will enhance the marine infrastructure available within Teignmouth Harbour by providing a dedicated safe access point for paddleboard and kayak users, reducing conflicts with other users at the existing slipway. This supports the harbour's role as a hub for maritime leisure activity on this section of the South Devon Coast.

policies.label: Infrastructure (SW-INF-2)
policies.policyInfo: (1) Proposals for alternative development at existing safeguarded landing facilities will not be supported. (2) Proposals adjacent and opposite existing safeguarded landing facilities must demonstrate that they avoid significant adverse impacts on existing safeguarded landing facilities. (3) Proposals for alternative development at existing landing facilities (excluding safeguarded sites) should not be supported unless that facility is
consideration: The pontoon will improve and enhance existing water access facilities at Teignmouth Harbour. It will provide an additional means of safe access to the water that is not currently available, directly benefitting recreational water users and our business operations.

policies.label: Ports, harbours and shipping (SW-PS-1)
policies.policyInfo: In line with the National Policy Statement for Ports, sustainable port and harbour development should be supported. Only proposals demonstrating compatibility with current port and harbour activities will be supported.
consideration: The project is located within Teignmouth Harbour which is managed by Teignmouth Harbour Commission as the harbour authority. We have consulted with the Harbour Master and our proposed pontoon location has been agreed in principle. The works will not obstruct any navigation routes or conflict with harbour operations. The pontoon will be clearly marked in accordance with harbour authority requirements.

policies.label: Ports, harbours and shipping (SW-PS-4)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals promoting or facilitating sustainable coastal and/or short sea shipping as an alternative to road, rail or air transport will be supported where appropriate.
consideration: This project does not have any direct relevance to coastal or short sea shipping. Teignmouth is a small leisure harbour and the proposed installation is within the main harbour basin away from any shipping channels.

policies.label: Renewables (SW-REN-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enable the provision of renewable energy technologies and associated supply chains, will be supported.
consideration: This project does not directly involve or affect any renewable energy infrastructure or activities.

policies.label: Air quality (SW-AIR-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals must assess their direct and indirect impacts upon local air quality and emissions of greenhouse gases. Proposals that are likely to result in increased air pollution or increased emissions of greenhouse gases must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - air pollution and/or greenhouse gas emissions in line with current national and local air quality objectives and legal requirements.
consideration: The project will have no impact on air quality. A small amount of machinery may be used during installation but any impact will be negligible, short term and localised.

policies.label: Biodiversity (SW-BIO-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enhance the distribution of priority habitats and priority species will be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on the distribution of priority habitats and priority species must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant d) compensate for significant adverse impacts that cannot be mitigated.
consideration: The project is not anticipated to have any adverse impact on biodiversity. Teignmouth Harbour is an existing built structure and the proposed pontoon location is within the main harbour basin which is regularly disturbed by vessel activity. A basic ecological assessment of the installation area has been carried out and no significant biodiversity features were identified.
The underside of the pontoon may over time provide some habitat for colonising invertebrates and algae which could provide a minor biodiversity benefit.

policies.label: Biodiversity (SW-BIO-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enhance or facilitate native species or habitat adaptation or connectivity, or native species migration, will be supported. Proposals that may cause significant adverse impacts on native species or habitat adaptation or connectivity, or native species migration, must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant d) compensate for significant adverse impacts that cannot be mitigated.
consideration: The project should have no adverse impact on native species. No piling or seabed penetration is involved so there will be no direct physical disturbance to the subtidal substrate.

policies.label: Biodiversity (SW-BIO-3)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that conserve, restore or enhance coastal habitats, where important in their own right and/or for ecosystem functioning and provision of ecosystem services, will be supported. Proposals must take account of the space required for coastal habitats, where important in their own right and/or for ecosystem functioning and provision of ecosystem services, and demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate d) compensate for - net habitat loss.
consideration: the proposed project should have no adverse impact on coastal ecosystems or habitats. The structure is small scale, reversible and does not require any modification to the existing coastline.

policies.label: Habitats (SW-HAB-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that incorporate measures to conserve deep sea habitats will be supported. Proposals that may have direct adverse impacts on deep sea habitats must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - direct adverse impacts on deep sea habitats.
consideration: This project will have no impact on deep sea habitats. The works are within a small enclosed harbour basin in the intertidal/shallow subtidal zone.

policies.label: Disturbance (SW-DIST-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on highly mobile species through disturbance or displacement must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant.
consideration: The installation works are short in duration (estimated 3-4 days on the water). No impact piling is involved - all fixings are to the existing harbour wall. Any underwater noise from drilling activities will be brief and localised.

Teignmouth Harbour is an active harbour with regular vessel movements. The works will not materially increase disturbance beyond existing background levels. We do not consider a marine mammal observer to be necessary given the nature and scale of the works but we will comply with any requirements set by the MMO.

policies.label: Invasive non-native species (SW-INNS-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that reduce the risk of introduction and/or spread of invasive non-native species should be supported. Proposals must put in place appropriate measures to avoid or minimise significant adverse impacts that would arise through the introduction and transport of invasive non-native species, particularly when: 1) moving equipment, boats or livestock (for example fish or shellfish) from one water body to another 2) introducing structures suitable for settlement of invasive non-native species, or the spread of invasive non-native species known to exist in the area.
consideration: All pontoon components and installation materials will be new and sourced from UK suppliers. There is therefore no risk of introducing invasive non-native species (INNS) from other waterbodies via the structure itself.
Installation will be carried out by a local contractor whose equipment will not have been used in other waterbodies prior to works at Teignmouth.

policies.label: Invasive non-native species (SW-INNS-2)
policies.policyInfo: Public authorities with functions to manage activities that could potentially introduce, transport or spread invasive non-native species should implement adequate biosecurity measures to avoid or minimise the risk of introducing, transporting or spreading invasive non-native species.
consideration: Once in place the pontoon will be used exclusively within Teignmouth Harbour. All hired paddleboards and kayaks will be rinsed with fresh water and inspected between sessions as standard operating procedure for our business. We will display Check Clean Dry signage at the pontoon.

policies.label: Marine litter (SW-ML-1)
policies.policyInfo: Public authorities must make adequate provision for the prevention, re-use, recycling and disposal of waste to reduce and prevent marine litter. Public authorities should aspire to undertake measures to remove marine litter within their jurisdiction.
consideration: The installation works will not generate any marine litter. Any packaging or waste materials generated will be removed from site and disposed of appropriately. We will provide a small waste bin on the pontoon for customers to use and will conduct daily checks.

policies.label: Marine litter (SW-ML-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that facilitate waste re-use or recycling to reduce or remove marine litter will be supported. Proposals that could potentially increase the amount of marine litter in the marine plan areas must include measures to, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - waste entering the marine environment.
consideration: the proposed project does not involve any activities that are likely to increase marine litter in the area. Our business promotes responsible use of the marine environment and we actively encourage customers to take all litter away with them.

policies.label: Marine protected areas (SW-MPA-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that support the objectives of marine protected areas and the ecological coherence of the marine protected area network will be supported. Proposals that may have adverse impacts on the objectives of marine protected areas must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate adverse impacts - adverse impacts, with due regard given to statutory advice on an ecologically coherent network.
consideration: The project site is within Teignmouth Harbour which is not itself within any marine protected area. The nearest designated sites are Dawlish Warren SAC, located approximately 4km to the north at the mouth of the Exe Estuary, and the Exe Estuary SPA and Ramsar site beyond that. The installation of a small floating pontoon within the existing harbour basin is not considered to have any pathways of impact to these or any other marine protected areas.

policies.label: Marine protected areas (SW-MPA-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enhance a marine protected area’s ability to adapt to climate change, enhancing the resilience of the marine protected area network, will be supported. Proposals that may have adverse impacts on an individual marine protected area’s ability to adapt to the effects of climate change, and so reduce the resilience of the marine protected area network,
consideration: the project will not have any impact on the ability of any MPA to adapt to climate change. The works are reversible and entirely within an existing built harbour structure.

policies.label: Marine protected areas (SW-MPA-3)
policies.policyInfo: Where statutory advice states that a marine protected area site condition is deteriorating or that features are moving or changing due to climate change, a suitable boundary change to ensure continued protection of the site and coherence of the overall network should be considered.
consideration: No impact on any Marine Conservation Zone features is anticipated. The nearest MCZ to Teignmouth Harbour is the Otter Estuary MCZ located approximately 12km to the east near Budleigh Salterton. The protected features of this MCZ are not present within Teignmouth Harbour and there are no pathways by which this project could affect them.

policies.label: Underwater noise (SW-UWN-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that result in the generation of impulsive sound must contribute data to the UK Marine Noise Registry as per any currently agreed requirements. Public authorities must take account of any currently agreed targets under the Marine Strategy Part One Descriptor 11.
consideration: No impact piling is proposed. The only potential source of underwater noise is from the use of an SDS drill to install fixing bolts into the harbour wall. This will be intermittent, of very short duration and is not considered sufficient to cause any disturbance to marine mammals or other mobile species.

policies.label: Underwater noise (SW-UWN-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that result in the generation of impulsive or non-impulsive noise must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise
consideration: As above - no significant underwater noise is anticipated from this project. The harbour is already regularly exposed to vessel noise and other anthropogenic noise sources.

policies.label: Water quality (SW-WQ-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that protect, enhance and restore water quality will be supported.
consideration: No materials will be introduced to the water column that could cause deterioration in water quality. All materials to be used are inert (HDPE floats, aluminium, galvanised steel, GRP). Any construction activities that involve working over water will use appropriate containment measures to prevent the accidental release of any materials.

policies.label: Access (SW-ACC-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals demonstrating appropriate enhanced and inclusive public access to and within the marine area, including the provision of services for tourism and recreation activities, will be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on public access should demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant.
consideration: The proposed pontoon will improve public access to the water within Teignmouth Harbour by providing a safe, stable and accessible boarding platform for paddleboards and kayaks hired to members of the public. The current access via the shared slipway is steep and can be slippery and access is often restricted when fishing vessels are using the same area.

The pontoon will include a level walkway from the gangway which will make access significantly easier for people of all abilities including those with limited mobility. We will display clear and appropriate signage.

policies.label: Employment (SW-EMP-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that result in a net increase in marine-related employment will be supported, particularly where they meet one or more of the following: 1) are aligned with local skills strategies and support the skills available 2) create a diversity of opportunities 3) create employment in locations identified as the most deprived 4) implement new technologies - in, and adjacent to, the south west marine plan areas.
consideration: South Devon Coast SUP Ltd currently employs two full-time members of staff and two seasonal staff during the summer months. The installation of the pontoon will improve our operational efficiency and safety which we expect will allow us to increase throughput of customers and generate additional revenue. This will support the case for increasing staffing in future seasons.
The project will also provide short-term employment for the local contractor carrying out the installation works.

policies.label: Fisheries (SW-FISH-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that support a sustainable fishing industry, including the industry's diversification, should be supported.
consideration: The pontoon will be positioned in the outer harbour away from the main fishing quay and slipway. We do not anticipate any negative impact on commercial fishing operations. We have discussed the proposed location with the harbour master and local fishing vessel operators and no objections were raised.
The pontoon may in fact reduce conflicts between recreational users and fishing vessels at the shared slipway during busy summer periods.

policies.label: Fisheries (SW-FISH-2)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enhance access for fishing activities should be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on access for fishing activities must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant. If it is not possible to mitigate significant adverse impacts, proposals should state the case for proceeding.
consideration: No impact on fisheries management or fish passage is anticipated. The pontoon is a floating structure with no seabed penetration and a minimal footprint of 32m². It will not obstruct any navigation channels or access routes used by fishing vessels.

policies.label: Fisheries (SW-FISH-3)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that enhance essential fish habitat, including spawning, nursery and feeding grounds, and migratory routes, should be supported. Proposals that may have significant adverse impacts on essential fish habitat, including spawning, nursery and feeding grounds, and migratory routes, must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - adverse impacts so they are no longer significant.
consideration: The project area is not within or adjacent to any known fish nursery areas, spawning grounds or migratory routes. Installation works are of short duration and low impact.

policies.label: Heritage assets (SW-HER-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that demonstrate they will conserve and enhance the significance of heritage assets will be supported. Where proposals may cause harm to the significance of heritage assets, proponents must demonstrate that they will, in order of preference: a) avoid b) minimise c) mitigate - any harm to the significance of heritage assets. If it is not possible to mitigate, then public benefits for proceeding with the proposal must outweigh the harm to the significance of heritage assets.
consideration: Teignmouth Harbour is a historic harbour but the proposed installation location is within the outer harbour basin rather than adjacent to any listed structures. We have checked the Historic Environment Record and are not aware of any heritage assets within the project area.
The harbour authority has confirmed they have no heritage concerns regarding the proposed location.

policies.label: Knowledge, understanding, appreciation and enjoyment (SW-SOC-1)
policies.policyInfo: Those bringing forward proposals should consider and demonstrate how their development shall enhance public knowledge, understanding, appreciation and enjoyment of the marine environment as part of (the design of) the proposal.
consideration: Our business is entirely focused on improving public access to and enjoyment of the marine environment. We provide paddleboard lessons for beginners and run family hire sessions throughout the summer season. We actively promote awareness of the marine environment and coastal wildlife among our customers and include a brief environmental induction as part of all our sessions.

The improved access that the pontoon will provide will allow us to extend our services to users who currently cannot access the water safely via the slipway, including older adults and families with young children.

policies.label: Seascape and landscape (SW-SCP-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals should ensure they are compatible with their surroundings and should not have a significant adverse impact on the character and visual resource of the seascape and landscape of the area. The location, scale and design of proposals should take account of the character, quality and distinctiveness of the seascape and landscape. Proposals that may have a significant adverse impact on the seascape and landscape of the area
consideration: The pontoon will be a small, low-profile floating structure that sits at water level and will be largely unobtrusive within the existing harbour environment. Teignmouth Harbour already contains various pontoons, buoys, moorings and vessels. We do not believe the addition of this structure will have any adverse impact on the character or visual amenity of the harbour or the wider seascape.
The structure will be finished in a neutral colour in keeping with harbour authority guidance.

policies.label: Tourism and recreation (SW-TR-1)
policies.policyInfo: Proposals that promote or facilitate sustainable tourism and recreation activities, or that create appropriate opportunities to expand or diversify the current use of facilities, should be supported.
consideration: This project directly supports and enhances tourism and recreational activity at Teignmouth. Our paddleboard hire and instruction business serves both local residents and the significant number of tourists who visit Teignmouth and the South Devon Coast each year. The improved access facilities will allow us to serve more customers safely and extend the season during which we can operate.
Teignmouth is a significant tourist destination and marine recreation is an important part of the visitor economy. The project is fully consistent with supporting this sector.
